title: Verify Server Hostnames during SSL/TLS Connections
description: This vulnerability allows attackers to impersonate a trusted host. When hostname validation is disabled, the client skips this critical check, creating an opportunity for attackers to pose as a trusted entity and intercept, manipulate, or steal the data being transmitted.
recommendation: Enable hostname validation during SSL/TLS connections
impact: Allows attackers to impersonate a trusted host, potentially leading to data interception, manipulation, or theft.

checks.message: Enable hostname validation during SSL/TLS connections to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks
